scope: Pass B read expansion for Harvest API v2 list/singleton endpoints that are expressible as bounded declarative streams. Legacy remains read-only; documented mutation endpoints are excluded with concrete permission/risk categories rather than exposed as broad reverse-ETL actions.

excluded.reason: Detail endpoints return the same object shape as the corresponding list streams; adding ID-parameter streams would require user-supplied IDs and would duplicate rows already available from the list endpoints.
Project-scoped task assignments are a filtered view of the covered /task_assignments stream.

These parameterized assignment endpoints require caller-supplied project/user IDs or fan-out from other streams, which is not expressible in the current declarative stream dialect without a StreamHook.
User rate/team endpoints expose compensation or manager relationship data and require administrator/manager permissions; they are intentionally outside the default read surface.
These nested endpoints require an invoice/estimate ID fan-out and are not expressible as standalone declarative list streams without a StreamHook.
Time reports require explicit from/to date windows and emit aggregate metric rows rather than source objects; adding them needs a separate report-parameter design, not a silent default range.
Report endpoints emit aggregate metric rows with report-specific required filters or permission-sensitive amounts; they are excluded pending a dedicated report-stream contract.

Harvest write endpoints create live business records and commonly require administrator or manager permissions; legacy Write is unsupported, so this read connector does not expose reverse-ETL mutations.
Patch endpoints mutate account settings or business records and require a dedicated reverse-ETL risk/approval design not present in the legacy connector.
Delete endpoints remove or archive live Harvest business data and are intentionally not exposed by this read-only connector.
